one_line_takeaway: By replacing a single fixed-configuration model-inversion solver with a three-agent LLM-driven closed loop (optimization with session branching/rollback, perception-based candidate scoring, and consistency-checked generative refinement), the authors show an eavesdropper can achieve >75% face-privacy leakage success against semantic communication systems even without instantaneous wiretap channel state information.
key_finding: The proposed agentic eavesdropper without wiretap CSI achieves FPESR > 75% at SNR >= 5 dB, versus both glass-box MIA baselines (with and without CSI) staying close to 0% FPESR at SNR = 0 dB. At SNR = 0 dB the agentic eavesdropper still attains an FPESR of 30-50% while both MIA baselines remain near 0%. The agentic eavesdropper without CSI nearly matches its own CSI-aware version and clearly outperforms MIA with CSI, indicating the agentic control loop substantially reduces dependence on instantaneous wiretap CSI. For SNR >= 10 dB, the full agentic eavesdropper achieves up to a 10 percentage-point FPESR gain over the generative-refinement-only ablation (which has CSI but lacks the agentic loop), and qualitative examples (Fig. 3) show the generative-refinement ablation hallucinates identity-inconsistent details whereas the full agentic pipeline preserves source identity/pose/attributes even without CSI.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is an LLM-orchestrated 'agentic eavesdropper' against semantic communication (SemCom) systems that use joint source-channel coding (JSCC). It reformulates the classical model-inversion-attack (MIA) optimization (joint recovery of the source image and the unknown wiretap channel matrix from an intercepted signal) as a closed-loop, LLM-driven sequential decision process with three cooperating agents: (1) an Optimization Agent that performs short-step (20-80 steps per call) gradient-based joint semantic-and-channel inversion within resumable, checkpointed 'sessions,' choosing among image-only, channel-only, or joint update modes and among continue/switch/rollback/kill actions, with tree-of-thoughts-style session branching when a trajectory stagnates; (2) a Perception Agent, a mixture-of-experts quality module combining no-reference image-quality metrics (NIQE, TOPIQ-NR-Face) with a multimodal LLM-as-judge that outputs structured JSON evidence (face presence, pose, artifact notes, confidence) used to score candidates since no ground-truth reference is available to the eavesdropper; and (3) a Refinement Agent that captions a promising-but-degraded candidate with a multimodal LLM, composes a restoration-only prompt (explicitly instructing the generator not to invent new content), and passes the output through an external generative image model, after which the result is handed back to the Optimization Agent to warm-start a fresh session and be accepted only if it remains consistent with the intercepted signal (otherwise discarded as a hallucination). This closed loop is the paper's novel mechanism; ReAct and Tree-of-Thoughts (cited to Yao et al.) and LLM-as-judge (cited survey) are explicitly credited as inspirations/prior techniques being adapted, not claimed as this paper's invention.
threat_model: Eve is a passive, glass-box eavesdropper: a former legitimate user who has access to the semantic encoder E_theta (used by the legitimate transmitter Alice) but not the semantic decoder D_phi. Eve intercepts the transmitted signal over an independent MIMO Rayleigh fading wiretap channel and, critically, does NOT have instantaneous channel state information (CSI) of the Alice-Eve link -- only its statistical distribution -- which the authors position as the practically realistic case versus prior work assuming instantaneous wiretap CSI. Eve's auxiliary knowledge K includes the semantic encoder, semantic priors, and an external generative model. The goal is to reconstruct a source image (face images from FFHQ) that is semantically/visually close to the original from the intercepted signal alone, without a reference image (hence no PSNR/SSIM available to Eve during the attack itself, only post-hoc for evaluation).
limitations: The paper does not state explicit limitations in a dedicated section; based on the described setup: (1) evaluation is restricted to a single dataset (FFHQ face images) and a single SemCom architecture (DeepJSCC with fixed BCR=1/12) and a single channel model (2x2 MIMO Rayleigh fading), so generalization to other content types, JSCC architectures, or channel models is untested; (2) the glass-box assumption (Eve has the encoder but not the decoder) is a specific and relatively strong threat model that may not hold in all deployment scenarios; (3) the attack pipeline depends on proprietary third-party LLM/generative services (Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Nano Banana 2), raising cost, latency, and reproducibility considerations not discussed; (4) no defense or mitigation evaluation is presented -- the paper's stated purpose is to expose the risk to motivate future secure SemCom design, not to propose or test countermeasures; (5) FPESR is defined via a single ArcFace-cosine threshold (0.7) which is one specific operationalization of 'successful' identity leakage.

quantitative_results: Proposed agentic eavesdropper (no wiretap CSI): FPESR > 75% for SNR >= 5 dB
Proposed agentic eavesdropper (no wiretap CSI): FPESR 30-50% at SNR = 0 dB
Glass-box MIA baselines (with and without wiretap CSI): FPESR close to 0% at SNR = 0 dB
Agentic eavesdropper without CSI achieves up to ~10 percentage-point FPESR gain over the generative-refinement-only (CSI-aware, non-agentic) ablation for SNR >= 10 dB
Success criterion: ArcFace cosine similarity > 0.7 defines a successful privacy leak (FPESR)
System config: 2x2 MIMO Rayleigh fading channel, SNR swept 0-20 dB, DeepJSCC with BCR = 1/12, Adam lr=5e-2 (image)/1e-2 (channel), TV weight 5e-4, 40 optimization steps per session call
baselines_compared: Eve: glass-box MIA with wiretap CSI
Eve: glass-box MIA without wiretap CSI
Eve: generative-refinement ablation with wiretap CSI but no agentic control loop
Eve: proposed agentic eavesdropper with wiretap CSI
Eve: proposed agentic eavesdropper without wiretap CSI (main proposed setting)
